com.tonic.jaloc.impl.stacks

Class PStructStack<T extends PStruct>

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.tonic.jaloc.memory.abs.AbstractNativeCollection<A,W>
      • com.tonic.jaloc.memory.abs.AbstractNativeList<PStructArray<T>,PStructWriter<T>>
        • com.tonic.jaloc.impl.stacks.PStructStack<T>
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.lang.AutoCloseable

    public final class PStructStack<T extends PStruct>
extends AbstractNativeList<PStructArray<T>,PStructWriter<T>>
    A growable native struct stack; elements are accessed through views, and any capacity change invalidates outstanding views.

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      PStructStack(NativeAllocator allocator, StructLayout layout, StructViewFactory<T> viewFactory, long initialCapacity)
      Creates an empty stack with the given starting capacity on the given allocator.
      PStructStack(StructLayout layout, StructViewFactory<T> viewFactory)
      Creates an empty stack with zero capacity on the system allocator.
      PStructStack(StructLayout layout, StructViewFactory<T> viewFactory, long initialCapacity)
      Creates an empty stack with the given starting capacity on the system allocator.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      StructLayout layout() 
      T peek()
      Creates a view of the top element.
      void pop()
      Removes the top element, zeroing its slot.
      T push()
      Pushes a zeroed element and returns its view.
      T push(java.util.function.Consumer<? super T> initializer)
      Pushes an element, initializes it, and returns its view; a throwing initializer rolls back.

      • Methods inherited from class com.tonic.jaloc.memory.abs.AbstractNativeList

        clear, ensureCapacity, trimToSize

      • Methods inherited from class com.tonic.jaloc.memory.abs.AbstractNativeCollection

        capacity, close, isEmpty, isOpen, size

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        equals, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • PStructStack

        public PStructStack(StructLayout layout,
                    StructViewFactory<T> viewFactory)
        Creates an empty stack with zero capacity on the system allocator.
        Parameters:
        layout - the entry layout
        viewFactory - creates the entry views

      • PStructStack

        public PStructStack(StructLayout layout,
                    StructViewFactory<T> viewFactory,
                    long initialCapacity)
        Creates an empty stack with the given starting capacity on the system allocator.
        Parameters:
        layout - the entry layout
        viewFactory - creates the entry views
        initialCapacity - the starting capacity
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if initialCapacity is negative

      • PStructStack

        public PStructStack(NativeAllocator allocator,
                    StructLayout layout,
                    StructViewFactory<T> viewFactory,
                    long initialCapacity)
        Creates an empty stack with the given starting capacity on the given allocator.
        Parameters:
        allocator - the allocator to source memory from
        layout - the entry layout
        viewFactory - creates the entry views
        initialCapacity - the starting capacity
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if initialCapacity is negative

    • Method Detail

      • layout

        public StructLayout layout()
        Returns:
        the entry layout

      • push

        public T push()
        Pushes a zeroed element and returns its view.
        Returns:
        the view
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if closed

      • push

        public T push(java.util.function.Consumer<? super T> initializer)
        Pushes an element, initializes it, and returns its view; a throwing initializer rolls back.
        Parameters:
        initializer - fills the fresh element
        Returns:
        the view
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if initializer is null
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if closed

      • peek

        public T peek()
        Creates a view of the top element.
        Returns:
        the view
        Throws:
        java.util.NoSuchElementException - if empty
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if closed

      • pop

        public void pop()
        Removes the top element, zeroing its slot.
        Throws:
        java.util.NoSuchElementException - if empty
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if closed
